input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd
m=pd.read_csv('market.csv', dtype={'qid':str})
print('rows',len(m),'active',(~m.is_resolved).sum(),'resolved',m.is_resolved.sum(),'no_pred_active',((~m.is_resolved)&(m.num_predictions==0)).sum())
active=m[~m.is_resolved].copy(); active['rd']=pd.to_datetime(active.resolution_date, errors='coerce')
for _,r in active.sort_values('rd').head(75).iterrows():
    print('\\n',r.qid, r.rd.date() if pd.notna(r.rd) else None, 'preds', r.num_predictions)
    print(r.title[:300])
    print('agg', r.market_aggregate)
PY"

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd
m=pd.read_csv('market.csv', dtype={'qid':str})
print('rows',len(m),'active',(~m.is_resolved).sum(),'resolved',m.is_resolved.sum(),'no_pred_active',((~m.is_resolved)&(m.num_predictions==0)).sum())
active=m[~m.is_resolved].copy(); active['rd']=pd.to_datetime(active.resolution_date, errors='coerce')
for _,r in active.sort_values('rd').head(75).iterrows():
    print('\\n',r.qid, r.rd.date() if pd.notna(r.rd) else None, 'preds', r.num_predictions)
    print(r.title[:300])
    print('agg', r.market_aggregate)
PY"

events.preview: Jan. 31 resolved the remaining end-of-month cluster: Alcaraz and Bad Bunny were correct late, while miners-bus was Ternivka and Balochistan was Nushki, both missed. I’m moving to the Feb. 1-3 markets now, starting with s

blocks.text: Jan. 31 resolved the remaining end-of-month cluster: Alcaraz and Bad Bunny were correct late, while miners-bus was Ternivka and Balochistan was Nushki, both missed. I’m moving to the Feb. 1-3 markets now, starting with strategic minerals, Norwegian corruption, market fire, Libya, League Cup, and the near-term legal/Ukraine questions.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,textwrap
from pathlib import Path
patterns=[
 ('strategic_minerals', ['strategic minerals','stockpile']),
 ('minerals_reserve', ['critical minerals','reserve']),
 ('norway_corruption', ['Norwegian','prime minister','corruption']),
 ('erna_corruption', ['Erna Solberg','corruption']),
 ('stoltenberg_corruption', ['Jens Stoltenberg','corruption']),
 ('market_fire', ['huge fire','market']),
 ('dhaka_fire', ['Dhaka','market','fire']),
 ('lagos_fire', ['Lagos','market','fire']),
 ('libya_haddad', ['Libya','Haddad','killed']),
 ('libya_killed', ['Al Jazeera Arabic','Libya','killed']),
 ('libya_forensic', ['Libya','forensic experts']),
 ('league_cup_final', ['League Cup final','Manchester City']),
 ('league_cup_arsenal', ['Manchester City','Arsenal','League Cup']),
 ('clippers_harden', ['Clippers','Harden','trade']),
 ('tyler_herro', ['Tyler Herro','Clippers']),
 ('brazil_bus_crash', ['Brazil','bus crash','16']),
 ('myanmar_panel', ['Myanmar','five-member panel']),
 ('ukraine_market_shelling', ['Ukraine','market shelling']),
 ('kostiantynivka_market', ['Kostiantynivka','market']),
 ('supreme_map', ['Supreme Court','congressional map']),
 ('louisiana_map', ['Louisiana','congressional map']),
 ('saleh_execution', ['Saleh Mohammadi','public execution']),
 ('lyon_deranque', ['Quentin Deranque','Lyon']),
 ('super_bowl', ['Super Bowl','Seahawks','Patriots']),
 ('gaza_troops', ['Gaza','8,000 troops']),
 ('lebanon_abducted', ['Israel','abducted','official','Lebanon']),
 ('narges_exile', ['Narges Mohammadi','exiled']),
 ('washington_post_publisher', ['Washington Post','Will Lewis','publisher']),
]
res=[]
with Path('articles/2026/01/31/articles.jsonl').open() as f:
  for i,line in enumerate(f,1):
    try:a=json.loads(line)
    except: continue
    text=(a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')
    low=text.lower()
    for label,terms in patterns:
      if all(t.lower() in low for t in terms):
        pos=min([low.find(t.lower()) for t in terms if low.find(t.lower())>=0] or [0])
        snip=text[max(0,pos-300):pos+1200].replace('\\n',' ')
        res.append((label,i,a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),a.get('title'),snip))
        break
from collections import defaultdict
counts=defaultdict(int)
print('matches',len(res))
for label,i,date,src,title,snip in res:
  if counts[label]>=8: continue
  counts[label]+=1
  print(f\"\\n## {label} line {i} pub={date} src={src}\\nTITLE: {title}\\nSNIP: {textwrap.shorten(snip, width=1400, placeholder=' ...')}\")
print('\\ncounts')
for k in sorted(counts): print(k,counts[k])
PY"

TITLE: ‘Batman’ confronts Californian city over ICE Super Bowl plan
SNIP: ‘Batman’ confronts Californian city over ICE Super Bowl plan LOS ANGELES – A man dressed as Batman berated officials in the California city set to host the Super Bowl in February over claims they could let US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers patrol the National Football League championship game. The caped crusader took to the mic during the public comments section of Santa Clara city’s council meeting on Jan 27 . He kicked things off with a “Ker-pow!” any superhero would be proud of, dropping an f-bomb as he slammed his fist into the lectern and demanded: “What... are we doing here?” “You have had months to prepare for this upcoming event,” he said of the game that will see the New England Patriots and the Seattle Seahawks battle it out for American football supremacy on Feb 8. “People are dying on our streets every single day in this country because we allow this federal government to walk all over you.” The outburst came with tensions running high in the United States over the deaths of two protesters in Minneapolis in January , both shot dead by federal agents in operations run by ICE and Border Patrol. He added: “You need to affirm that no city resources will go to ICE, that no cooperation will be given

## league_cup_arsenal line 549 pub=2026-01-30 src=www.straitstimes.com
TITLE: Arteta shrugs off title pressure ahead of Arsenal's trip to stubborn Leeds
SNIP: Arteta shrugs off title pressure ahead of Arsenal's trip to stubborn Leeds Jan 30 - Mikel Arteta is keen not to let Arsenal's three-match winless streak in the Premier League affect the players, calling for his team to stay grounded as they pursue their first English top-flight title since 2003-04. Sunday's home loss to Manchester United shaved Arsenal's lead at the top to four points over Manchester City and Aston Villa. But Arteta said that Arsenal should focus on the overall picture of their season so far. "It's about understanding where you are," Arteta told reporters on Friday, a day before their trip to Leeds United. "Certainly when you lose a game you have a lot of feelings, especially this group of players because they are so competitive. They search for excellence and when you don't reach it, you ask questions, and we did that. "My role there is to bring optimism and reality of where we are. Our club has a long, long history and to find the position we are in February, it's hard to find." Arsenal, who beat Kairat Almaty 3-2 on Wednesday, won all eight of their Champions League matches, reaching the round of 16 as the top team in the league phase. The north Londoners are also gunning for the FA Cup and the League C

TITLE: Tottenham vs Manchester City Prediction, Betting Tips & Odds
SNIP: Tottenham vs Manchester City Prediction, Betting Tips & Odds The pressure will be back on Tottenham boss Thomas Frank on Sunday when they face Manchester City at home in the late kick-off (4:30pm, live on Sky Sports). They might have qualified for the last 16 of the Champions League with a 2-0 win over Eintracht Frankfurt and finished fourth in the league table, but in the Premier League, they sit 14th, and the fans are less than impressed. The reaction to Frank has been toxic at times as he struggles to win over the Spurs faithful, but maybe a win over City could help? Despite all the speculation and pressure on Frank, he is not the favourite with the football betting sites to be the next manager to lose his job; that honour goes to Liverpool boss Arne Slot, who is 4/6 while Frank is 2/1. City also made it through automatically to the knockout stage with a 2-0 win at home to Galatasaray, to join Arsenal, Liverpool and Chelsea safely through. Neither side is going into the game in the best of form domestically, and City have won just one of their last five games in the league and that came last time out against the bottom side Wolves. Before that, they lost to Manchester United and drew with Su
